title: Navarretia demissa
taxonomy: Navarretia demissa is classified in the following way: it belongs to the family Polemoniaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the "jacob's ladder" or "gloved poppy" family, and it is specifically placed in the genus Navarretia. This plant is part of the order Ericales. Within the kingdom Plantae, Navarretia demissa is a member of the subkingdom Viridiplantae and the phylum Angiospermophyta. The species is also placed in the class Magnoliopsida, a class of flowering plants that have two or more cotyledons. It is a dicotyledonous plant, which means it has seeds with two cotyledons.
uses: Navarretia demissa, a plant native to North America, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues. Its leaves and flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties. In some Native American cultures, the plant is used to treat skin conditions and wounds.
